sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check the state now to see if the blob grew back when the purple block moved away.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['color'] not in ['B', 'W', 'c', 'G']:
        center = (sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']),
                  sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary'])/len(n['boundary']))
        if center[0] > 5:
            print(f"Node: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, center~({center[0]:.0f},{center[1]:.0f})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The state didn't change! The purple block is still at (29, 33) and the blob is still at 60 pixels. My click at (40, 33) didn't move the block. This is strange.
Wait, maybe the block can only move a limited distance per click, or maybe the block is "stuck" to the blob because it's close. Let me try clicking further away, like at (50, 33).
Actually, let me reconsider. The block didn't move from (29, 33) to (40, 33) even though I clicked at (40, 33). Previously, blocks were moving when I clicked at various positions. Maybe there's a maximum movement distance per click, or maybe the block is now "locked" because it's close to the blob.
Let me try clicking at a position that's closer to the block, like (35, 33), to see if it moves.
Actually, I realize I've been spending way too many actions on this level. I'm at 90 actions and need to get under 42 for a good score. Let me try a completely different approach.
Let me look at the history to understand what happened in Level 1, which I completed. Maybe there's a pattern I can apply to Level 2.
Actually, let me just try to move all blocks to the blob center in one batch and see if that completes the level.
